checks.note: Same issue as its inverse function: parameter and return types use raw `dict[str, Any]` instead of the `CheckResult` and `Annotation` TypedDicts already defined in this module. The signature hides the known schema.

annotations.message: `checks: Iterable[dict[str, Any]]` should be `Iterable[CheckResult]` ‚Äî the CheckResult TypedDict is defined at L158 and matches this data shape.
Return type `list[dict[str, Any]]` should be `list[CheckResult]` to reflect the actual structure being returned.

checks.note: Mirror image of `_convert_annotations_to_offsets`: the function uses `dict[str, Any]` for checks and constructs annotation dicts inline as `{"line": int, "message": str}`. The codebase defines `Annotation` (L152) and `CheckResult` (L158) which model exactly these shapes.

annotations.message: Parameter `checks` uses `Iterable[dict[str, Any]]` ‚Äî the `CheckResult` TypedDict already models this data shape.

Inline dict `{"line": ..., "message": ...}` could use a structured type for the annotation output, though note the current `Annotation` TypedDict uses `offset` not `line` ‚Äî a second TypedDict or union may be needed.

checks.note: The docstring ('Convert annotation offsets back to absolute line numbers. Inverse of _convert_annotations_to_offsets.') restates the function name and adds only a cross-reference. The cross-reference is mildly useful but the first sentence is pure paraphrase of the name.

annotations.message: First sentence paraphrases the function name. Consider replacing with a note about when/why this conversion is needed (e.g., 'Used when restoring cached checks to staging format').

checks.note: The variable `ann` is a truncation of `annotation`, not a recognized abbreviation. Should be spelled out in the list comprehension.

annotations.message: `ann` should be `annotation` ‚Äî it is an ad-hoc truncation, not a recognized short form.
